channel.title: Comments for page "Our Mutual Guide"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Our Mutual Guide"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, and yet on reflection they've put the bones down by then. I've replaced it with:

The man who these bones belonged to, he knew this place too.

Hopefully that will suffice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well geez, this is a belated answer, but yes, it would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would this work better, mlister:

The man whose bones they carry knew this place too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The man whose bones these are knew this place too.

I absolutely cannot parse this sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, great! Wonderful to see this get put together. I like the format now, too; I remember there being a big question about how to put the pieces of this story together. You did it great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The prose is gorgeous, the visions have this sense of twisted, faded grandeur to them, and your depiction of 012's compulsion effect is downright contagious - like your antagonist, I find myself yearning to see the piece completed.

Well done. +1

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
The next tale in the On Mount Golgotha series. It is time for this story to have an antagonist, although sometimes it's not clear who is the antagonist and who is the protagonist.

In terms of understanding the musical references, I suggest starting with Bartok's Music for Strings, Percussion and Celesta - you can listen to the first movement here.

In terms of understanding the imagery, I suggest turning down the lights, turning up the Bartok and reading again :)

Many thanks are due, and I will no doubt need to be reminded of people I've missed. Thank you to Hercules Rockefeller and sirpudding in particular for some excellent feedback on the forums. Thanks also to Jekeled, Decibelles and others who provided crit. And of course to WrongJohnSilver, who not only started this whole fun thing, but also put up with my pestering to get the Hub posted so I could add this!
